entity_types.name: ResearchPaper
entity_types.description: A scholarly article published in a conference or journal.

attributes.name: publication_date
attributes.description: Date when the paper was published

attributes.name: conference
attributes.description: Conference where the paper was published

entity_types.name: University
entity_types.description: An educational institution where the scandal takes place.

entity_types.name: Department
entity_types.description: A specific academic division within a university.

attributes.name: university
attributes.description: University to which the department belongs

entity_types.name: Scandal
entity_types.description: An event involving allegations of wrongdoing within an institution.

entity_types.name: MediaOutlet
entity_types.description: An organization that disseminates news to the public.

attributes.name: focus
attributes.description: Primary focus or area of coverage

entity_types.name: SocialMediaPlatform
entity_types.description: An online platform where users can share opinions and information.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Name of the social media platform

attributes.name: user_base
attributes.description: Description of the platform's user base

entity_types.name: Investigation
entity_types.description: A formal inquiry into the allegations of the scandal.

entity_types.name: StudentAssociation
entity_types.description: An organization representing the interests of students.

entity_types.name: Person
entity_types.description: An individual involved in the scandal.

attributes.name: role
attributes.description: Role or position of the person

entity_types.name: Organization
entity_types.description: A group or institution involved in the scandal.

edge_types.name: AUTHORED_BY
edge_types.description: Indicates the person who authored a research paper.

edge_types.name: PART_OF
edge_types.description: Indicates the department to which a person or event belongs.

edge_types.name: REPORTED_BY
edge_types.description: Indicates the media outlet that reported on the scandal.

edge_types.name: DISCUSS_ON
edge_types.description: Indicates the social media platform where the scandal is discussed.

edge_types.name: INVESTIGATED_BY
edge_types.description: Indicates the organization responsible for investigating the scandal.

edge_types.name: SUPERVISED_BY
edge_types.description: Indicates the person who supervised another person's work.

analysis_summary: The text describes a plagiarism scandal at Wuhan University involving a professor and a graduate student. Key figures include the accused professor, the student who raised the allegations, a journalist, and university officials. The scandal unfolds over several months, with significant media coverage and discussions on social media platforms. The ontology captures entities such as research papers, universities, departments, and social media platforms, as well as relationships like authorship, supervision, and media reporting.
